## Email Services

True Wealth Financial uses LPL as their Email provider.
LPL's Email services integrates with Microsoft Exchange.
C22 does not have any management ability of these systems beyond basic troubleshooting.
Trevor Dietrich has the ability to request account creations for new employees.
Upon account creation, users are sent a few different credentials.
1. First.Last@lpl.com
2. First@twfinancial.com
3. Password to sign in.
Users can sign in at advisormail.lpl.com using the first.last@lpl.com and the password.
That re-routes them to their First@twfinancial.com account and signs them into OWA.
A document explaining Outlook configuration settings is in the TWF SPO under Docs and Manuals.

## Phones (Teams)

TWF is using Teams for their Phone system.
They have Yealink MP56 Teams phones on their desks, signed into their individual Teams accounts.
Phone licensing:
Teams Phone with calling Plan (Counrty zone 1 – US)(About $15/mo)
Resource Account licenses:
Microsoft Teams Phone Resource Account (Free license for resource accounts)
Their main phone line is 303-452-5200.
Calls placed to the main line work as follows
1.Call to 5200 is placed
a.Call connects to "TWF Auto Attendant" (This Auto Attendant is assigned to the "True Wealth
Financial" resource account)
b.Auto Attendant checks business hours
c.If inside of business hours forward to Call Queue (Assigned to the "TWF Call Queue"
resource account)
i.If outside of business hours, forward to Shared Voicemailbox (TWF Voicemail
Forwarding)
d.This will ring all 3 users in the office at the same time, if they are available.
Voicemail is set up as follows
1.Voicemail is left at the main line (5200)
a.Voicemails are sent to the "TWF Voicemail Forwarding" O365 group.
b.Voicemails can be checked on the web at this link:
https://outlook.office.com/mail/group/twfinancial.com/TWFVoiceMailForwarding/email
c.Voicemails can be checked by members of the Call Queue by going to the "Voicemails" tab
on their Teams app or Teams Phone.
i.Users MUST be Exchange Online enabled to view voicemails.
They have a First Orion (T-mobile's database) account to manage their caller ID (creds in LP)

WireGuard VPN Setup  
1.Remove old VPN configuration from Adapter Settings in Control Panel  
2.Configure User Client object in Unifi  
a.Open Unifi Console > VPN > Target WG VPN  
b."Add Client" > Input user's name > Download the configuration file > Add  
c.Make sure to "Apply Changes" at the bottom-left  
3.Edit Configuration file with notepad  
a.Update the "Allowed IPs" in the \[Peer\] section to: AllowedIPs = 192.168.19.1/24, 10.0.0.1/24  
i.Note: If you downloaded the config file multiple times and its name is appended with  
(1), (2), etc. You must remove that from the name or WireGuard will throw an error.  
4.Install WireGuard on User's Machine  
a.Windows installer found here: Installation - WireGuard  
b.Import Config file into Wireguard  
c.Activate and test  
i.Note: Confirm user can ping Target Group Gateway. 10.0.0.1  
5.Educate user on change in process for activating VPN  
a.Wireguard, when activated, will remain active even after a computer restart.  
b.If for some reason it is not active, they just need to open the Wireguard application via the  
red icon in the taskbar and click "Activate".  
c.They must deactivate the VPN while in-office to prevent issues accessing company resources

## Foundation

  
Here are the instructions for accessing Foundation Cloud from any new machine:  
1.Open a browser and navigate to [https://foundationsoftware.onelogin.com](https://foundationsoftware.onelogin.com)  
2.Enter your email address as your username  
3.Enter the password for Foundation Cloud (not the internal Foundation database password)  
4.If prompted, enter the code texted to your phone. This step may sometimes be skipped for  
previous logins  
5.Once logged in, click on the Foundation tile in the middle left of the screen  
6.The page will attempt to start the App Controller software but will fail the first time because it is  
not yet installed  
7.Click the option to Restart the App Controller (this step may sometimes not be necessary)  
8.Click the option to Download the App Controller  
9.The App Controller software will download to the browser downloads. Click on it to run it.  
10.The installer will pop up with a picture of two women in front of a computer. Click the Install  
Option  
11.Once install is complete, click the “Close” option  
12.Click the Restart App Controller option again on the web page.  
13.If prompted by the browser, click to Allow the plug in to run. Select the checkbox to always allow  
You should see the small grey box open up after about 20 seconds if this process worked.

## VPN Services

STEAMe's Azure services are access-restricted to their office's IP address. To accommodate
remote-work, a WireGuard VPN has been configured via their UniFi gateway to allow
employees a tunnel into the office's network.
Static WAN IP: 198.0.243.62
VPN Subnet: 192.168.3.1
Modem details: Comcast modem is not in bridge mode. UniFi has a static IP set at 10.1.10.39,
and a DMZ has been configured.
Instructions for configuration & installing VPN for users
WireGuard should be available through MDM (Apple Business Manager)
Check App Store > User name (bottom left) > find WireGuard
If not there, install WireGuard from website: Installation - WireGuard
You'll need to go to the UniFi console and create a config file for the individual user's tunnel
endpoint
After downloading config file, you do not need to edit it. 0.0.0.0/0 for Allowed IPs will work for
SteamE's purposes.
Wireguard Install
Get the config file to the end user's computer, open WireGuard, select Add Tunnel near bottom
left, and add the config file to the client.
Once imported, click Activate to start VPN
After activation, have the end-user navigate to portal.azure.com to make sure the tunnel is
working correctly.
Users may also be part of a security group in Staging Database SSH Tunnel that may need to be
deactivated. This tunnel is looking for their home IP address to allow access to SteamE
resources. Since they are no longer using their home address, this may conflict with their access
when VPN is activated. - 7/22/25
VPN Background Info
As mentioned, this is a WireGuard VPN. Info about its specific configs are in unifi
Comcast Business Modem is at WAN IP 198.0.243.62
DMZ rule on, host 10.1.10.39

DMZ rule on, host 10.1.10.39
198.0.243.62:51820
This is the port exposed for tunneling. Firewall is otherwise enabled and controlled through
unifi network controller

ABM Primer:
Devices need to first belong to STEAMe's ABM:
If they're purchased from Apple directly through STEAMe's purchasing partner, devices should
arrive connected to ABM
If a device cannot be found in ABM, we need to add it to their account.
This can only be completed with the computer during factory reset
You will need someone physically present with Apple Configurator downloaded on a mobile
device
During computer setup when asked to select country, point AC scanner at screen for
adoption
After device is confirmed in ABM, you will need to enroll device in Apple Business Essentials
ABE is what allows the device to adopt policies, apps, and more
Upon setting up computer, user can then enroll device in MDM via Settings > General > Device
Management > Sign into work or school
If a device is setup for auto-enrollment (not active as of 2/27/26), it should enroll straight out of the box
TLDR: If a device can't be found in ABM, adopt it via Apple Configurator (download to mobile device and
then factory reset the Mac device. Use AC scanner when asked to select country on Mac.)
Once available in ABM, ensure device is assigned Apple Business Essentials. To enroll device in MDM,
user needs to create local profile on Mac, then sign into Work or School via Settings > General > Device
Management.

## Maverick

Thursday, August 28, 2025 12:32 PM

### Maverick Access & Authentication Guide

READ THIS FIRST SECTION BEFORE PROCEEDING

### Section 1: Overview & typical sign-in flow

Maverick is an internal application developed by the GoTickets team. Access requires two layers of authentication:  

-   1\. Cloudflare Zero Trust (ZT) – Provisioned automatically based on group membership, using Entra ID as the identity provider.
-   2\. Maverick Account– provisioned manually by Mike Piacenza (as of 08/28/2025). This uses their Entra user object for sign-in, but is not automatically provisioned at this time. We now have access to view existing users in Maverick via this link [https://admin.gotickets.com](https://admin.gotickets.com) (use our Skybox Microsoft creds) and add new users see instructions below for adding a new user (1/20/2026 NG).

Typical sign-in flow:  

-   User navigates to Maverick application via a web browser: [https://admin.gotickets.com](https://admin.gotickets.com)
-   User is redirected to a Cloudflare Zero Trust authentication page.
-   ○User selects "Azure AD" to authenticate with Cloudflare. This will initiate a sign-in to their Entra ID account. They will need to pass all checks as is typical with an Entra/O365 sign- in.
-   ▪ntra/O365 username/pass, and MFA. If they haven't configured MFA, they'll be prompted to do so.

-   User is redirected to the Maverick sign-in page.
-   User should select "Sign In With Microsoft" to access the application.

Application URL: [https://admin.gotickets.com](https://admin.gotickets.com)  
SOP for application access: Reference: GoTickets Maverick MFAv2.pdf  
This is provided to new user's via GoTicket's management

### Section 2: Providing internal users access

Application URL: [https://admin.gotickets.com](https://admin.gotickets.com)  
Account creation:  

-   1.Confirm user has an Entra (O365) account.
-   2.Confirm user has access to the appropriate security group(s). Alison Eubanks and/or Mike Piacenza can provide specifics which groups to apply.
-   a. Security group membership automatically provisions Cloudflare Zero-Trust access.

```
These are the groups that exist for this purpose:
Contractor groups:
○SG-GoTickets-ZT-External Maverick Admin
○SG-GoTickets-ZT-External Fraud Agents
○SG-GoTickets-ZT-External Staging Env
Internal groups:
○SG-GoTickets-ZT-Internal Maverick Admin
○SG-GoTickets-ZT-Internal Fraud Agents
○SG-GoTickets-ZT-Internal Staging Env
```

-   3\. GoTickets management will provide an SOP for application access when necessary. This provides the end-user with details on the sign-in flow and authentication setup. 
-   a. SOP: [GoTickets Maverick MFA.pdf](https://c22tech.sharepoint.com/:b:/g/EUqWmHVlZFVKjpzmSlxjnGsBucAYIyytuKCvyylEDwzbOw?e=CiuNuE)

-   4\. Create a Maverick Account for the users (Check section 4)

### Section 3: Contractor Account Creation Flow

1.GoTickets requests C22 to invite contractor(s) as guest users. They often make requests for  
new off-shore contractors and don't mention guest users.  
○GoTickets will specify the email address to invite, and the appropriate security group(s)  
based on required Maverick access.  
2.C22 sends the guest invite and configures group membership as requested.  
a.Note: Security groups apply CloudFlare Zero-Trust permissions.  
3.C22 provisions Maverick account if not already created. (Check section 4)  
a.SOP dictates GoTickets will provision Maverick accounts before requesting contractor  
access, C22 needs to add Azure User Principal Name after invite  
4.GoTickets provides contractors with instructions for sign-in. We have a copy of these  
instructions in our SharePoint, located here: GoTickets Maverick MFA.pdf  
a.Please read these instructions so you understand the process.  
Many of their contractors will have external accounts and language barriers, so clarity on the sign-in  
and authentication flow is important.

### Section 4: New User Creation (3/25/26 NG)

1.Sign into Maverick platform by going to Home | Go Tickets (use our Microsoft Skybox creds to sign in).  
2.Once in, go to Users > Add User  
3.All required fields should be filled in.  

-   a.Username: User FirstInitialLastName
-   b.FirstName
-   c.LastName
-   d.Phone - Not required
-   e.Email (Company or guest user email)
-   f.AWS Whitelisted IP (Leave this blank)
-   g.Time Zone (Use best judgement. Central when unsure. Many guests will be international)
-   h.User Status: Typically will use "Active"
-   i.Roles: Always choose only "USER" unless specifically instructed otherwise.
-   j.Supervisor: Choose the user's supervisor. Should be the person who is reaching out to the HD, but ask if unsure. This is important as it kicks of a notification to the supervisor.
-   k.Azure user Principal Name: This is the Entra UPN. It's important that this is correct, as it is used to sign into the platform via SSO. Find this by going to Entra > Users > User > User Principal name will be here. Guest users will look different, like this for example alexandrabizios\_gmail.com#EXT#@SkyboxCapital.onmicrosoft.com
-   l.InContact Agent ID: Leave this blank

4.If an error is presented upon creating the user which indicates the user may already  
exist, you can go to the "View Users" tab on the left and search for the user.  
Sometimes the Azure Principal (UPN) is wrong, or access is not applied.

### Section 5: Troubleshooting Access Issues

There are typically two points of failure for access:  
First: Failure to authenticate with Cloudflare Zero-Trust.  
Second: Failure to authenticate with the Maverick application.  
Basic steps:  
1.Confirm with the user at which point this failure is happening.  
a.A part of this step is confirming the correct sign-in method is being used by the end-user:  
○Cloudflare ZT: use the “Azure AD” button (user/password entry will not work).  
○Maverick: use the “Sign in with Microsoft” button (user/password entry will not  
work).  
2.Confirm the user is part of the Skybox Entra tenant (normal or guest user).  
3.Confirm the user is a member of one of the security groups listed in section 2.  
4.Confirm MFA is enabled via Microsoft Authenticator or SMS for their Entra ID.  
Where is the access failure happening?  
1.Cloudflare: Proceed to section 5.1 below  
2.Maverick: Proceed to section 5.2 below  
Section 5.1:  
1.Does the user have the correct security groups assigned?  
2.Is the user using the "Azure AD" button to sign-in?  
3.Do you see sign-in logs on their account for this application?  
4.Is MFA configured on their account?  
5.If they're a contractor with a guest account, ask them to try from an InPrivate browser session.  
Section 5.2:  
1.Is the user utilizing the "Sign In with Microsoft" button?  
2.What error are they receiving?  
3.Confirm with Joe Parenti that the user's account is configured and allowed for Microsoft sign-in  
for Maverick.  
a.If user has account in Maverick, ensure Azure User Principal Name is filled  
b.If it is not filled, confirm with Quintina ([quintina@gotickets.com](mailto:quintina@gotickets.com)) the accounts are OK for  
login  
4.If all else fails for contractors with guest accounts, delete the guest object and recreate.  
Confirm security groups prior to deletion.  
5.In rare cases, utilizing a different email address for contractors may be the best solution. While  
this is not strictly necessary, the language barrier and time-zone differences with some  
contractors make properly troubleshooting this issue untenable. Guest access to the Skybox  
environment causes confusion for some users, especially when they have Google, Microsoft,  
and other O365 access under one email address already.

## VPN Services

VPN URL  
Ro.c22tech.com  
Connect to VPN with C22Admin ( The Domain Admin )  
Today, R Olson has two VPN servers, both configured via VPN settings in their Unifi console.  
1.C22 L2TP Server  
a.This is an older technology on its way out. We are moving away from this VPN as users experience problems,  
but we're not actively reaching out to reconfigure to the new method.  
b.Instructions to set this up are at the bottom of this page under "L2TP VPN Setup"  
2.C22 WireGuard Server  
a.This is a Wireguard VPN configured via the Unifi console, and is our preferred VPN method moving forward.  
b.This VPN connects the client device to a separate subnet, so RO resources must be accessed using the FQDN,  
not the NetBIOS name.  
i.For example, a user using this VPN cannot connect to \\rovmfs2024, but can connect to  
\\rovmfs2024.ro.local  
c.This VPN uses split tunneling. This means that the client device only uses the VPN connection when accessing  
R Olson resources, resulting in a faster general connection to websites and other external resources.  
WireGuard VPN Setup  
1.Remove old VPN configuration from Adapter Settings in Control Panel  
2.Configure User Client object in Unifi  
     a. Open Unifi Console > VPN > C22 WireGuard Server  
     b. "Add Client" > Name after the user > Download the configuration file > Add  
     c. Make sure to "Apply Changes" at the bottom-left  
3.Edit Configuration file with notepad  
     a.Update the "Allowed IPs" in the \[Peer\] section to: AllowedIPs = 192.168.87.1/24, 192.168.21.1/24  
i.Note: If you downloaded the config file multiple times and its name is appended with (1), (2), etc. You  
must remove that from the name or WireGuard will throw an error.  
Install WireGuard on User's Machine  
4.  
a.Windows installer found here: Installation - WireGuard  
b.Import Config file into Wireguard  
c.Activate and test  
i.Note: You cannot test this if the end-user's device is connected to the R Olson network already  
5.Educate user on change in process for activating VPN  
a.Wireguard, when activated, will remain active even after a computer restart.  
b.If for some reason it is not active, they just need to open the Wireguard application via the red icon in the  

b.If for some reason it is not active, they just need to open the Wireguard application via the red icon in the  
taskbar and click "Activate".  
c.They must deactivate the VPN while in-office to prevent issues accessing company resources  
L2TP VPN Setup  
1.Confirm user has a profile in Unifi VPN settings  
a.Log into R Olson Unifi console  
b.Settings > VPN > C22 L2TP Server  
c.Under "User Authentication" confirm a user exists. If not, create one  
d.Account name should match the user's domain UPN  
e.Password should match the other accounts that are already set up (Click the eye to confirm)  
2.VPN Configuration on end-user machine  
a.Network and Sharing Center > Change Adapter Settings on left-hand side > Delete existing "RO VPN" object, if  
it exists  
b.Network and Sharing Center > Set up a new connection or network > Connect to a workplace > Use my  
internet connection (VPN)  
c.Internet address: ro.c22tech.com  
d.Destination Name: RO VPN  
e.Check Allow other people to use this connection > Click OK  
f.Change adapter settings on the left side of Network and Sharing Center > Properties for RO VPN  
g.  
3.Security Tab  
a.Type of VPN: Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol with Ipsec (L2TP/Ipsec)  
i.Click Advanced Settings > use preshared key for authentication > input Pre-Shared key from Unifi VPN  
settings  
ii.Data encryption: Require encryption (disconnect if server declines)  
iii.Allow these protocols: Microsoft CHAP Version 2 (MS-CHAP v2)  

iii.Allow these protocols: Microsoft CHAP Version 2 (MS-CHAP v2)  
iv.  
4.Networking tab  
a.Uncheck IPv6  
b.Leave IPv4 settings as is  
5.System Tray > VPN > Connect  
a.Use Credentials you configured in Unifi in step 1

Brock handles this. ### Claude Desktop – Deployment Quick Guide Step 1 Enable Required Windows Features Use LogMeIn One2Many for zero-touch deployment or, run an elevated Powershell session via RDP For One2Many, use the task with this name: Claude\_Phase1\_DeveloperMode\_and\_VMplatform\_Enable If you want to do an RDP session instead, you can run these commands manually: Enable Virtual Machine Platform: Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName VirtualMachinePlatform -All –NoRestart If you get an permissions error try: dism.exe /online /enable-feature /featurename:VirtualMachinePlatform /all /norestart Enable Developer Mode: reg add "HKEY\_LOCAL\_MACHINE\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\AppModelUnlock" /t REG\_DWORD /f /v  "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1" Step 2 Stage the Installer Use One2Many file distribution or the LogMeIn file browser to copy Claude.msix to: C:\\Temp\\Claude.msix Task name is: Claude\_Phase2\_Distribute\_Claude.MSIX Step 3 Install Step 3 will not work via One2Many. You must run this via Command/Powershell through LogMeIn, or RDP. Add-AppxProvisionedPackage -Online -PackagePath "C:\\Temp\\Claude.MSIX" -SkipLicense -Regions "all" Verify Confirm machine-wide provisioning: Get-AppxProvisionedPackage -Online | Where-Object { $\_.DisplayName -like "_Claude_" } Confirm VirtualMachine Platform is on: Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName "VirtualMachinePlatform" Confirm Developer Mode is on: Get-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\AppModelUnlock" -Name "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" ### Debtwire Email [help@debtwire.com](mailto:help@debtwire.com) and CC Kurt Hommen ([kurt.hommen@acuris.com](mailto:kurt.hommen@acuris.com) & [kurt.hommen@iongroup.com](mailto:kurt.hommen@iongroup.com)) with New hire's name, title, and email, and location. UPD4PCL6Win81P_2400MU.zip c451i Printer Vendor: Pacific Office Automation Contact: Nick Leko (nick.leko@pacificoffice.com) ## Conference Rooms Old Name Old Email NewNew Email Name DAL-Large ConferenceDAL.LargeConferenceRoom@pppllStar DAL.Star@pppllc.com Roomc.com DAL-Huddle 1 DAL.Huddle1@pppllc.com Crescent DAL.Crescent@pppllc.c om DAL-Huddle 2 DAL.Huddle2@pppllc.com Ranger DAL.Ranger@pppllc.co m DAL-Huddle 3 DAL.Huddle3@pppllc.com Trinity DAL.Trinity@pppllc.co m ## Camera and Doors Door controller is installed by D&D Security David@dd-security.net The controller is an Avigilon Controller, and reaching out to AWS servers. If we run into Firewall issues, here's a link to documentation on whitelisting & ports. Also copy/pasted below in case this site changes the URL. What are the Network Requirements for Avigilon Controllers? - External Help Center - Confluence (atlassian.net) Firewall An Ethernet connection with DHCP must be used to connect the Smart Hub (ACU) or Single Door Controller (SDC) to the Local Area Network (LAN). You must also configure firewall settings to communicate with the Avigilon Alta system. Avigilon Alta uses the following outbound ports: •TCP port 443 •UDP port 123 For the Video Reader Pro and Video Intercom Reader Pro, to obtain the best audio and video quality, we also recommend allowing access to the outbound UDP ports listed below. Additionally, please ensure UDP hole-punching is enabled (or disable symmetric NAT). •TCP port 443 •UDP port 123 •UDP port 3478 - TURN/UDP servers that assist in establishing connectivity •UDP 50000 - 60000 - UDP connection for WebRTC Note: Fortinet firewall has antivirus software pre-configured to run on the firewall. That Antivirus blocks all AWS traffic by default. AWS will need to be whitelisted. Note: If using an external DNS server, outbound UDP port 53 must also be open. To support Wi-Fi unlocking from the mobile app, the ACU/SDC's inbound TCP port 443 must be available from within the LAN. Inbound port forwarding on the router, firewall, or NAT device is unnecessary. We do not allow HTTPS certificate rewriting or TSL/SSL inspection. IP Address We do not provide an IP range or FQDN list of Avigilon Alta hostnames for Basic and Premium licenses. Most hostnames resolve to dynamic IPs and the hostnames themselves change during provisioning and configuration update processes. If you wish to segregate traffic from your Avigilon controllers, you can enable a DMZ for the controllers to separate their traffic. Static Cloud IP For Enterprise Licenses we offer a Static Cloud IP. This allows organizations with strict network firewall policies to easily open only a few IP addresses to allow Alta Access hardware (ACU/SDC) to connect to the cloud. (ACU/SDC) to connect to the cloud. This release includes a new version of the Openpath Admin app, which allows for the provisioning of ACU/SDC devices behind an already restricted firewall. Video Reader / Video Intercom Reader network security best practices Protecting edge devices •PoE access control readers, like the Video Reader Pro / Video Intercom Reader Pro, require a wired network connection installed on the unsecured side of a door. To remove the risk of an attacker gaining access to the local network via the network connection if they’re able to remove the reader from the wall, Avigilon Alta recommends that your IT team place the exposed Ethernet port on a demilitarized zone (DMZ) or perimeter network. Improving network reliability •To improve reliability during some network outage conditions (such as a router outage), you should set static IPs on both the Video Reader Pro and its Remote ACU(s). ## Set email-to usersemailaddress@pepc.org Set two-factor email Next End ### Section 3: Installing & Signing into FortiClient 1.Download and install FortiClient VPN to the user's machine. Download link: Product Downloads | Fortinet Product Downloads | Support a.NOTE: When downloading, make sure you select "Forticlient VPN only" as the download option. Other installers will install additional bloat and may not function as intended. https://www.fortinet.com/support/product-downloads#download-vpn-only 2.Configure a new VPN Connection a.Select SSL-VPN as the type b.Connection name: PEPC VPN c.Description: N/A d.Remote Gateway: 50.239.137.250 e.Check "Customize Port" and input 10443 f.Leave SSO Unchecked f.Leave SSO Unchecked g.Authentication: Prompt on Login (changed 12/3/25) h.Username: User's AD username (first initial, last name) i.NOTE: Username is case sensitive. i.Client Certificate: None 3.Password: User should input their AD password. ### Section 4: Troubleshooting: •Most commonly, access issues will be due to invalid credentials. If the user changes their AD password, they must input the new password into the FortiClient VPN application before connecting. If they've selected "Save Password", they probably don't know they need to change it. •Confirm the user's Forticlient VPN is configured correctly as outlined above. ## Printers RICOH Downstairs: 10.10.0.50 | User: admin Pass: Ricoh MP C3504 Upstairs (10.10.): 10.10.0.49 | User: admin Pass: Ricoh IM C6000 Mod Building: 10.10.0.48 | User: pepcprntadm Pass: lf_NDNjlGY5jqIeB Scan to email: pepcscanner@pepc.org Pepcscanner2@pepc.org (backup recovery account) SMB scanning: scanuser (on pepc.local) Pacific Office Automation Upstairs Ricoh IM C6000 Equip. M2AM CRM Application Summary
The CRM system is a web based tool created by C22Tech and Chris Mollet. It is
hosted at XNet on C22WebBasic and can be accessed at the web address
http://app.m2am.com. The base page processes all emails waiting with
business cards while the actual web interface can be found at
http://app.m2am.com/admin
New business cards and contacts can be added to the system by taking a photo and
attaching it to a mail item sent to m2amcontact@C22tech.net . The subject of the
email should be LastName, FirstName of the contact to be created. When the
main page is hit (once every 15 minutes) the contact should be created
automatically. If you browse to the home page at http://app.m2am.com you will
see the contacts that it's attempting to process based on what has been emailed to
it. To stop SPAM posts users must send the email from an address tied to a user
created in the CRM admin area already.
New users for CRM are created by logging into http://app.m2am.com/admin and
creating a new user under the Administration tab.
The mail account M2AMContact@c22tech.net is hosted as part of MailEnable on
Dallas and is accessed by the website via IMAP from XNet to Dallas.
Never use salutations when creating a contact.

M2AM CRM Application Summary
The CRM system is a web based tool created by C22Tech and Chris Mollet. It is
hosted at XNet on C22WebBasic and can be accessed at the web address
http://app.m2am.com. The base page processes all emails waiting with
business cards while the actual web interface can be found at
http://app.m2am.com/admin
New business cards and contacts can be added to the system by taking a photo
and attaching it to a mail item sent to m2amcontact@C22tech.net . The subject
of the email should be LastName, FirstName of the contact to be created.
When the main page is hit (once every 15 minutes) the contact should be created
automatically. If you browse to the home page at http://app.m2am.com you will

automatically. If you browse to the home page at http://app.m2am.com you will
see the contacts that it's attempting to process based on what has been emailed
to it. To stop SPAM posts users must send the email from an address tied to a
user created in the CRM admin area already.
New users for CRM are created by logging into http://app.m2am.com/admin and
creating a new user under the Administration tab.
The mail account M2AMContact@c22tech.net is hosted as part of MailEnable on
Dallas and is accessed by the website via IMAP from XNet to Dallas.
Never use salutations when creating a contact.

Storage Details
Drives Size Type Storage Pool
Drive 1 7.3TB HDD 1 – RAID1
Drive 2 7.3TB HDD 1 - RAID1
Drive 3 1TB HDD 2 -RAID1
Drive 4 1TB HDD 2 -RAID1

Use Cases
1. Google Workspace backup
The Active Backup for Google Workspace service runs a continuous task to backup all of
Insightpsinc and InsightBPT's Shared Google Drives to the Synology.
2. Scan sync to Google Drive
Their office scanner scans to \\INSIGHTNAS\Scans
The "Cloud Sync" Package running on the Synology periodically scans this folder and pushes
new files to their Google Drive tenant > Shared Drive named "Scans"
A script runs daily which deletes anything in this folder older than 7 days.
Details and instructions for staff can be found in our SharePoint here: Scanning to Google
Drive.docx
Scans folder clean script:
#!/bin/bash
find /volume1/Scans -name "*.*" -type f -mtime +7 -exec rm -f {} \;

It will integrate your Zoho account in Outlook, providing for a streamlined user experience. To install the app on your computer, follow these directions: -Browse to www.zoho.com and click on the “Login” link in the upper right hand corner of the window. -Type, in the appropriate fields, your email and password for Zoho. -After logging in, you’ll see a screen with multiple icons. Under the subcategory “Our Most Popular,” click on the CRM icon and it will take you to a new page. -On this new page, there is a tool icon in the upper right hand corner of the window. It will have a wrench and screwdriver in an X pattern. Click on this icon. -From the dropdown menu, choose “Setup.” This will take you to another page. -On the Setup page, there will be multiple options. Click on the option “Others” under the subcategory “Extensions and APIs.” -From there, the Plug In for Microsoft Outlook should be available. Click on “Continue” underneath the Outlook Plug In. -In the next screen, there will be a dropdown menu to “Choose your Microsoft Outlook Version.” Choose “Microsoft Outlook 2010, 2013 & 2016 32-Bit,” unless you know you are using either a 64-bit version or Mac Outlook. -Once selected, the download button should appear. Click on this download button. -The download should start and when finished, run the downloaded file. It will take you through the installation steps. -You should be able to click “Next” on each step of installation to finish. There are no special steps for installation. -Restart Outlook and the Zoho Add In will be in the right hand side of Outlook. Sign into your Zoho account with your Zoho credentials and you should be able to integrate your Zoho account into Outlook. From OLD Page 35

clients.FPC Packaging: # FPC Packaging ## General Tech Info and Notes ### Oak Brook Office ProLiant MicroServer Gen8 •Old machine that they want to confirm we can log into. Once we log into it we can shut it down. Issues 12/11/23 Excel files sync issue Crystal Skipworth ### Morton Grove Office Currently under construction 12/12/2023 so there is not much equipment here. ## SALESFORCE INFO This customer uses Salesforce pretty extensively. Presently their assorted SF orgs are controlled by Joe Greer. KEY TERMS: Salesforce – massive platform with lots and lots of functions, even it's own coding language (APEX. It's java-like but with a sql style syntax for querying SF's ponderous relational DB). TBH if you're reading this and don't know what SF is that's fine, but google it before continuing. Sandboxes - salesforces lower environments where admins/devs can test new features before deploying them to prod Orgs – individual instances of Salesforce. A tenant is a single billed customer. A tenant may have many orgs. Each org is a collection of records & the rules that govern them Every 6 months or so, their sandbox org will refresh. This wipes the DKIM key of the salesforce org. Some of the automation functionality of Salesforce allows people to send email from the platform or set up system generated emails from the platform. These system generated emails need DKIM keys need to be set up internally in the platform itself or most business' email servers will not deliver the mail to the recipient. Instructions for doing so are here: Create a DKIM Key Salesforce SPF and DKIM FAQ They use GoDaddy as their registrar, our canteratech account has delegate access. Updated 2026-01-29 AB ## Teams Call Flow ForeFront uses Microsoft Teams as their phone line provider. They use two auto attendants, a call queue, and 3 resource accounts for their call flow. Below is an outline of the flow. 1.Call comes into their main line at 312-573-5686 and is connected to the "FPC Auto Attendant". There main of 708-836-1105 is still managed by Comcast. a.Hours for this line are M-F 8:30am – 4:00pm b.If the call is during normal business hours, the call is redirected to the Reception Call Queue. c.The Reception call queue rings to Amalia Kanelos first, and directs to Laurie Jones if Amalia is offline or not available. d.If no one answers, the call is redirected to a shared voicemail. i.The Voicemail is left on a 365 group called ForeFront Voicemail Forwarding ii.A recording and transcription of the VM is forwarded to a shared mailbox called ForeFront Voicemail (forefrontvoicemail@forefrontscs.com) iii.To have access to voicemails, a user just needs access to the shared mailbox. 2.If the call comes in outside of normal business hours, it's redirected to another auto attendant called "FPC Auto attendant before-hours" a.This AA has available hours of 7:00am-8:15am M-F. b.If the call comes in during those times, they can press 1 to speak with Jose Cruz. The purpose of this is for delivery drivers to contact the warehouse. c.They can also press 2 to leave a voicemail. d.If it is not within those available hours, the call is redirected to voicemail. ## Email Services Office 365 ## DNS Summary All Logins and passwords should be kept in LastPass, not this document Domains used by client: forefrontscs.com Fpcpackaging.com Registrar's Used by Client: GoDaddy DNS Hosted Location: GoDaddy is host and we have delegate access. ## Public Website Information Where is the public website hosted? Is the website builder listed in Vendors ## Technology Vendors All usernames and passwords should be kept in LastPass, not this document Website Builder: Copier Vendor: ISP Vendor (Account number): Comcast Account Number:8771101320380567 Phone Service Vendor: Microsoft ## VPN Services none ## Servers Retired servers are offline but located in the office. ## Computer Preferences Purchasing and setup considerations for PCs and users Most users have Microsoft Surfaces. ## Backup Summary ## Software Used ## Data Loader for Sales Force Link to download: Download and Install Data Loader | Data Loader Guide | Salesforce Developers Instructions: ### Accessing the Data Loader Download To get started, you need to download the Data Loader application. It’s always best practice to get the latest version, and Data Loader no longer bundles Java with its installer. Since Data Loader version 45 and above now requires users to install Zulu OpenJDK, let’s start with that. 1.If you’re on Windows OS, download Azul Zulu from this link. The file should end with .msi. If you’re using MacOS, the appropriate installer can be found here and the file should end with .dmg. 2.Double click the installer file to run the setup wizard. 3.Follow the wizard instructions and hit Finish or Close. 4.Go to Salesforce Data Loader to download the latest version of Data Loader, which is updated with every Salesforce release. The download button is usually at the top of the page. 5.Once the download is complete, open the zipped folder and select Extract All. 6.In the folder of extracted files, look for the installation file. There should be two files named installer.bat and installer.command. Use installer.bat if you’re on Windows OS, and installer.command if you’re using MacOS. 7.Run the installation file. 8.When asked to provide the installation directory, just hit Enter to accept the default name “dataloader”. 9.Respond with Yes or No when it asks if you want a desktop shortcut or a Start Menu shortcut. 10.Data Loader will start installation (this usually goes very fast, mine barely felt like a second!) and the next message will automatically be “Data Loader installation is quitting.” Press any key to fully exit the command interface. ## FBC Alhua DVR & Camera notes  
Called Ahlua support 877-606-1590 opened ticket # 0130124066. Told them I was Chad Frederick .  
They walked me through the DVR reset. Since FBC did not now the account email or passcode or  
questions, we had to perform a factory reset of the Ahlua DVR. This is done by removing the DVR cover,  
removing the power cable, holding the black reset button on the motherboard and repowering the DVR  
while holding the button till it beeps ( I did this) after reset, I set the account up as follows  
The main contact email is yours "[cfrederick@4fbc.org](mailto:cfrederick@4fbc.org)” and I had to set up 3 security questions, so see a  
snapshot of these below.  
I was also required to create an "admin logon pattern", so I did, and it looks like the image below.  
Set up 2 users  
User = admin  
Password = F2o0o2t3hills

Password = F2o0o2t3hills  
I also set up your email as the default and set up the security questions as follows;  
I recommend you keep these ADMIN credentials private as “your back door” should you ever need it.  
The other credentials are as you requested.  
User = [safetyandsecurity@4fbc.org](mailto:safetyandsecurity@4fbc.org)  
Password = SENTRYSECURITY@#&!  
For the remote techs to log in, I had to download the remote support program “Anydesk”, and then  
open the Anydesk app to get the nine-digit address of the laptop. I then shared this code with the tech  
who "remoted in" and took control  
The next step was to search / find the cameras, and the support tech who remoted in could not find  
them on the TCP/IP network. Since this did not work, he instructed me to perform a factory reset on the  
cameras.  
Upon inspection, these are a model 50230UNI-A and they way to reset them is to remove the clear  
camera bubble 3 TORX 15 Screws that retained by the cover (they don’t fall out).Next step is to remove  
the 4 #1 Phillips screws that fasten the black plastic camera cover to the camera base. These are not  
retained and will come loose, so be careful.  
Once this black rounded cover is off, you can see the brass reset button (labeled K3) on the camera  
board next to the J2 terminal block on one corner of the board. (see photo below) The board actually  
just above the PTZ camera body, so you will be looking up at the bottom of this board  
Note there is also a board up in the base of the camera support assembly, but the K3 switch is on the  
board inside the actual camera housing. .  
After the reset, the DVR still cannot find the camera, so the on the next support call, the tech advised  
me to physically connect (Ethernet) to the POE switch in the server room to see if they can remote in  
and find the cameras.  
The good news is they were successful finding and configuring these cameras as channels 1 & 2. These  
cameras are DHCP, so their IP address changes every time the DVR I’d reset, or power cycled.

Should you ever need the camera passwords they are the same as your admin password.  
One last thing, you asked if the camera could be reversed ( which makes sense) and this can be done but  
would require connecting your laptop via ethernet to the main POE switch, open the AnyDesk app and  
the call the Ahlua support team. They can remote in and use the “configurator tool app” ( I allowed  
them to load this on your laptop) to switch the camera IPS to the other channels. Not impossible, just  
time consuming. Up to you

Data Request Tracking Process 

-   Determine whether the request is ongoing (recurring) or a one-time data request. 
    
-   Select the appropriate tab in the Tracker spreadsheet.
    

 Complete all required fields in the tracking sheet as outlined below: 

Name 

-   Use the name of the request. 
    
-   Example: IP-4193 – FEB Data Request
    

 Braviant Owner 

-   Reporter, Example: IP-4193 – Vincent Zhang (Reporter)

 External Owner 

-   Example: IP-4193 – FEB (as listed in the request title)

 Type of Feed 

-   Indicate whether the request is recurring or one-time.

 Schedule if Recurring 

-   Should be listed under "Control Description"

 Jira Ticket 

-   Enter the corresponding Jira ticket number

 Status 

-   Change/update as information is provided by the requestor

 PII (Personally Identifiable Information) 

-   Indicate if applicable; specified in the Jira request under "Description of Shared PII"

 Method of Transfer 

-   Refer to the Jira ticket for details

 Date of Request 

-   Use the date the Jira ticket was created

 Date Completed 

-   Update once the requestor confirms completion

 Production Confirmed By 

-   Enter the approver’s name listed on the Jira ticket  
      
    From ticket, it will be unclear who is actually sending out the data. You will need to ask for this clarification in JIRA ticket.

### Atlassian/Jira

Jira is a platform within Atlassian  
Google SSO with [c22admin@braviant.com](mailto:c22admin@braviant.com)  
Braviant.atlassian.net

**Helpdesk tickets** can be created by emailing **ithelpdesk@braviantholdings.com**

Stephen & Lucas review JIRA tickets on Wednesday. Chad Hoyt reviews on Thursdays. C22 meets with Braviant on Fridays.

  
About Jira  
Jira is a ticketing platform focused on incident tracking. It is what Braviant are using to create helpdesk  
tickets.  
Some features of the platform include:  
•The ability to communicate with internal team directly within the ticket  
•Ability to communicate with non-IT user population within the ticket  
•Ability to link documents/documentation related to the ticket directly to the ticket itself  
•Monitor time to ticket resolution and adjust stages of progress for ticket  
rd  
•Link directly with other Atlassian/3party saas platforms (most notably Confluence, Atlassian's  
knowledge base product)  
•High level of indexability. We can and should be tagging incidents with types as we work them, so  
we can quickly/easily reference these for similar incidents in the future  
Updated by AB 1/8/26

  
Internal Note vs Reply to Customer  
Internal notes are not visible to a lot of parties, especially anyone who is involved with the Call Center  
In general, if you need to communicate with one of the people who we need to grant us access, (usually  
found in the 'People and Contacts' page of OneNote) then internal notes are better.  
If you need to communicate with the requester/reporter of a ticket, reply to customer is better  
Updated by AB 2026-01-27

a HITRUST-certified, HIPAA-compliant email security solution that provides "zero-step"  
encryption for healthcare organizations

steps by the sender or recipient, which prevents human error.  
•Seamless Integration: Works directly within existing email clients like Gmail and Outlook.

### ThreatDown

**ThreatDown is the endpoint monitoring system that Braviant uses (anti virus)**

Necessary for SOC-2 compliance

Creds are in LP via [helpdesk@c22tech.com](mailto:helpdesk@c22tech.com)  
OTP with DAITO

**When detections emerge**, please send to [josh.anderson@echeloncyber.com](mailto:josh.anderson@echeloncyber.com) & [christopher.tomei@echeloncyber.com](mailto:christopher.tomei@echeloncyber.com)

Echelon Cyber (CISO) wants to continue monitoring things closely until they have better grasp on Braviant's ecosystem

**Key Terms**  
**Nebula**: Nebula is the actual EMS that handles the technical security ThreatDown provides.  
Nebula has MDR-like capabilities, as do many EMS. As the M implies, it is primarily used  
for monitoring, and has a series of dashboards that allow for oversight of individual devices as  
well as things like configuring firewalls.

This is an example of one of the main dashboards in the Nebula console.  
Nebula architecture – ThreatDown Support Portal

**OneView**: OneView is the admin panel that broadly covers the full range of actions/services  
that are available to Threatdown admins: integrations, deployments, billing, nebula platform  
mgmt, etc.**bold text**

## Hexnode/Tablets

Any tablet that needs to be re-enrolled will first need to be disenrolled from Hexnode.
Disenrollment process
(Skip this section if this is a new tablet with factory settings)
1.Sign into https://apipennsylvania.hexnodemdm.com/
2.Click Manage > locate the tablet and click on it
3.In the top right corner click “Actions” > “Disenroll Device”
4.Wait about 5 minutes
5.Go to “Manage” and check if the device is still in the list.
6.If so, click on it and select “Actions” > “Mark as Disenrolled”
Once disenrolled, we can reset and re-enroll.
Re-enrollment/enrollment process
1.Reset the tablet to factory settings
2.While resetting, sign into https://apipennsylvania.hexnodemdm.com/
3.Navigate to “Admin” at the top > Android Enterprise on the left-hand side
4.You should see “Device Owner Settings” with a QR code below. Leave this open on your laptop
5.Once the tablet finishes resetting, DON’T sign into it
6.Tap the welcome screen 6 times
7.Sign into Wi-Fi as prompted
8.Scan the QR code and follow the prompts to enroll the device.
The “Android 7.0 or later” section on this site shows step-by step instructions on enrollment once you’ve
scanned the QR code.
https://www.hexnode.com/mobile-device-management/help/how-to-enroll-a-device-in-android-in-the-
enterprise-as-device-owner-using-hexnode-mdm/#

START > Settings > Network & Internet > VPN > Add a VPN Connection
Provider: Windows Built-In
Connect Name: AJC VPN
Server Name: AJC.C22Tech.com
VPN Type: SSTP
Username: User's UPN
Password: User's Domain Password
--- Confirm VPN Settings ---
Control Panel > Network and Sharing > Change Adapter Settings >Select AJC VPN > Properties
General Tab:
Confirm VPN Address: AJC.C22TECH.COM
Security Tab:
Type of VPN: SSTP
Authentication: Use Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP), EAP-MSCHAP v2
Properties: Automatically use Windows Login (Only if device is joined to on-prem domain)
Networking Tab:
IPv4> Properties > Advanced > Uncheck "Use Default Gateway..."

## VPN Services

A4CB has two VPN's:  
1.A client VPN used by A4CB staff to access network resources like shared drives while away from the  
office.  
2.A site-to-site VPN with an AWS VPC environment owned by Loanwell, one of their business  
partners.  
If a user is reaching out about VPN issues, it is almost certainly the client VPN, not the Site-to-site.  
Refer to the sub-pages of the VPN Services category here for more information.

## Client VPN (WireGuard)

VPN Type: Wireguard  
VPN Server: A4CB/Hatchery firewall  
VPN WAN: 12.37.253.106  
A4CB clients connect to a wireguard VPN (hosted on the A4CB/Hatchery Unifi network), which gives them access to office  
resources.  
Wireguard VPN quick-notes  
This WireGuard VPN server lives on the UniFi Firewall "A4CB/Hatchery". To connect, user's must have WireGuard installed,  
have a "Client Profile" created on the UniFi Firewall, and have a config file generated from that profile placed on their  
machine.  
Individual user profiles can only be used on ONE machine at a time. If a user has multiple computers, we must generate  
another config file for them.  
Wireguard, when activated, will remain active even after a computer restart. WireGuard configs are accessible across  
device profiles.  
If it is not active, they just need to open the Wireguard application via the red icon in the taskbar and click "Activate".  
Educate users on this process, if they don't already know.  
WireGuard VPN Setup  
1.Remove old VPN configuration from Adapter Settings in Control Panel (If applicable, won't be necessary on new  
devices)  
2.Check if the user has a User Client object in UniFi. Create one if not.  
a.Open Unifi Console for A4CB/Hatchery > Settings > VPN > VPN Server > A4CB  
b.Check client list for user's name. If it exists, a config file should be stored in C22's SharePoint already.  
i.C22 Team Site > A4CB > Client VPN > WireGuard Configs  
c.If a user doesn't already exist, "Add Client" > First & Last Name > Download the configuration file > Save the  
Config file to the SharePoint location defined above  
Make sure to "Apply Changes" at the bottom-left. If you don't do this, you'll have the config file but the user  
d.  
won't exist on the VPN server.  
3.Edit Configuration file with notepad (If you had to create the user)  
a.Update the "Allowed IPs" in the \[Peer\] section to: AllowedIPs = 192.168.25.0/24  
i.Note: If you downloaded the config file multiple times and its name is appended with (1), (2), etc. You  
must remove that from the name or WireGuard will throw an error.  
4.Install WireGuard on User's Machine  
a.Windows installer found here: Installation - WireGuard  
b.Import Config file into Wireguard  
c.Activate and test  
i.You can test by pinging the domain controller:  
Troubleshooting:  
If the error "WireGuard is running, but the UI is only accessible from desktops of the Builtin Administrators group" is  
presented, follow these steps. (Or, skip to the end and run the two commands via elevated command prompt)  
•Sign into an Administrator profile on the device  
•Open regedit as an administrator  
○Navigate to HKLM\\Software\\Wireguard  
▪Note: If a Wireguard key (folder) does not exist under HKLM\\Software, right click software > New > key >

▪Note: If a Wireguard key (folder) does not exist under HKLM\\Software, right click software > New > key >  
name it Wireguard  
○Open the Wireguard key folder > Right click > New DWORD  
○Name it: LimitedOperatorUI  
○Set the value to 1  
○Close regedit  
•Add the non-privileged user in question to the Network Configuration Operators group via CMD  
○Open CMD as administrator  
○Run this command: NET LOCALGROUP "Network Configuration Operators" AD\\username /ADD  
▪Note: AD\\Username is the local username you want to run WireGuard as. For example, a4cb\\jdoe  
•Sign out of the Administrator profile on the device and back into the user's profile  
•WireGuard should now run and open  
Note: If you're comfortable with the command line, you can skip the first few steps and run this from an elevated command  
prompt:  
REG ADD HKLM\\Software\\WireGuard /V LimitedOperatorUI /T REG\_DWORD /D 1 /F  
NET LOCALGROUP "Network Configuration Operators" AD\\username /ADD  
Note: AD\\Username is the local username you want to run WireGuard as. For example, a4cb\\jdoe

### Part 1

### VPN Setup Instructions

Note: If you're setting up a new device or device that is not domain joined and you are not connected to  
the A4CB network, refer to Part 2 of this article first.  
1.Sign into device as an Administrator  
2.Confirm device is joined to the A4CB.org local domain  
3.Run A4CBMerakiVPN.ps1 as Administrator  
1.This script is in our SharePoint at C22 Team Site\\A4CB\\Client VPN\\A4CBMerakiVPN.ps1  
Script details: This script does the following:  
•Defines VPN Parameters (Connection Name, Server Address, PresharedKey)  
•Checks for rasphone.pbk in system  
•Creates rasphone.pbk if missing  
•Removes any existing VPN connection with the same name to avoid conflicts  
•Adds a new VPN connection using:  
○Tunnel type: L2TP  
○Authentication: PAP  
○Scope: All Users (Available to all users on this device)  
•Enables split tunneling  
○Allows traffic to go through the VPN only for specified subnets: '192.168.24.0/24',  
'192.168.25.0/24','172.16.0.0/24', '10.255.255.0/29'  
•Creates a desktop shortcut  
4.If the script is stored on the user's device, DELETE IT after running it. The script contains a pre-shared  
key which should not remain on the user's device in plain text.  
5.If you are receiving error that scripts cannot be run, open Powershell and run the following:  
•Get-ExecutionPolicy –List  
•Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned –Scope CurrentUser  
○  
This should allow scripts to be run.  
•Run Powershell ISE as admin. Open script. Run script.

### Part 2

### VPN Setup for Devices not joined to the local domain

Sometimes you may have a need to connect to the VPN from a device that is not yet joined to the local  
domain. If you're setting up a new laptop from a remote location, for example.  
1.Create new Windows VPN with steps below:  
1.Windows Settings > VPN Add VPN  
a.Connection Name: A4CB VPN  
b.Server Name or Address: a4cb-accion-wireless-appliance-btvjcdzdjjc.dynamic-m.com  
c.VPN Type: L2TP/IPsec with pre-shared key  
d.Pre-Shared Key: Copy the pre-shared key in LastPass under "A4CB Client VPN PreShared  
Key"  
e.Type of sign-in info: Username and password  
f.Leave Username & Password fields blank  
g.Uncheck "Remember my sign-in info" (We will be replacing this VPN object after it's joined  
anyway)  
2.Open Control Panel > Network and Sharing Center > Change Adapter Settings > A4CB VPN  
Properties  
a.Security tab:  
i.Check: "Allow these protocols"  
ii.Check: "Unencrypted password (PAP)"  
iii.Uncheck: Microsoft CHAP Version 2 (MS-CHAP v2)  
3.Connect to VPN using AD credentials. You can use our domain admin during setup(C22admin with  
server creds), these credentials should be cleared from VPN config before handoff.  
4.Once the device is domain joined, use the script in the "VPN Setup Instructions" in the first part of

for smooth operation.

## (OLD) Client VPN Troubleshooting

As of 01/8/2026 this article is deprecated.  
If a user's VPN is not working, confirm the error received when attempting to connect and follow steps  
below.  
VPN Error: The remote connection was denied because the user name and password combination you  
provided is not recognized, or the selected authentication protocol is not permitted on the remote  
access server  
This is a credentials issue. The user is using incorrect credentials or their credentials have expired and  
must be reset. Since their device can't reach the DC, they don't receive a reset prompt.  
1.Sign out of the device (Go back to Windows sign-in)  
2.Click the VPN connect option in the bottom-right corner  
3.Ask the user to sign in using their credentials. If it connects, it will sign them in and prompt a  
password reset (If the password is expired)  
a.Confirm the user knows their password, is signed into their applications, and can see  
network drives  
4.If it doesn't connect, sign in using our Domain admin.  
a.Once connected, open Command Prompt and type "nslookup" without the quotes. Hit  
enter.  
b.Confirm the Default Server is MAIN-DC01.ad.a4cb.org or MAIN-DC02.ad.a4cb.org  
c.Switch Users (Don't Sign out. We want the VPN to stay active under our Domain Admin)  
d.Sign into the user's Windows profile. They should receive a password reset prompt here.  
Once reset, they'll be good to go.  
e.IMPORTANT: Make sure our Domain admin credentials are wiped from the VPN cache  
before disconnecting from the machine.  
i.Windows Settings > Network & Internet > VPN > A4CB VPN > Advanced Options >  
CLear sign-in info  
5.If this doesn't resolve, there may be a routing issue. Continue to next step.  
6.Sign into user's Windows profile & copy over the A4CBMerakiVPN.ps1 script from our Sharepoint  
(C22 Team Site\\A4CB\\Client VPN)  
7.Run script as Admin  
8.Repeat steps 1 through 4

## Site-to-site VPN with Loanwell

### Site-to-Site VPN Documentation – A4CB ↔ Loanwell AWS VPC

### Overview

A site-to-site IPsec VPN is in place between A4CB’s on-premises environment (Meraki MX85 firewall) and  
Loanwell’s AWS-hosted VPC environment.  
The VPN is used to provide secure, private connectivity between internal resources at A4CB (subnet  
192.168.25.0/24) and Loanwell systems inside their AWS VPC (10.0.0.0/16).  
This VPN allows Loanwell staff and systems to communicate with specific A4CB's SQL server as if they were  
on the same local network, bypassing the public internet.  
A4CB, nor C22, have access to Loanwell's AWS VPC environment. They are aware of this.  
Loanwell Point of Contact: Sean Doherty ([sean@loanwell.com](mailto:sean@loanwell.com))

### Documents:

A config file provided by AWS is located in our SharePoint at C22 Team Site > A4CB > VPN (Site-to-site) >  
Config from AWS > vpn-04fc163e7c14eb34e.txt  
This config provides details on how the IPsec tunnel should be configured specifically for Meraki MX devices.  
A word doc containing AWS VPC static routes, security groups, and NACL rules is located in our SharePoint at  
C22 Team Site > A4CB > VPN (site-to-site) > AWS\_VPN\_Technical\_LW-Notes.docx  
This doc explains the static routes and security rules Loanwell should have configured within their VPC  
environment, as of 09/02/2025.

### Endpoints and Network Details

VPN Hosting Details:  
•Device: Cisco Meraki MX85  
•WAN Public IP: 12.37.253.106  
•Local Subnet(s): 192.168.25.0/24  
•VPN Type: Policy-based IPsec (Non-Meraki peer)  
VPN Settings  
•Encryption Domains (interesting traffic):  
•Local: 192.168.25.0/24  
•Remote: 10.0.0.0/16  
•IKE/IPsec Proposals:  
•Phase 1: AES-256, SHA1/SHA256, DH14, lifetime 28800s  
•Phase 2: AES-256, SHA1/SHA256, PFS14, lifetime 3600s  
•Redundancy: Two AWS tunnels provided. MX supports only one active at a time, so only one is configured.  
During maintenance, there will be outages.  
•Routing (A4CB Perspective)  
•Path from 192.168.25.0/24 to 10.0.0.0/16:  
○Client on 192.168.25.x sends traffic to default gateway 192.168.25.1 (local router).  
○  
Router forwards 10.0.0.0/16 traffic to the Meraki MX at 10.255.255.6.

○Router forwards 10.0.0.0/16 traffic to the Meraki MX at 10.255.255.6.  
○MX encrypts the traffic and sends it across the VPN tunnel.  
○Encrypted traffic reaches Loanwell’s AWS VPC (10.0.0.0/16).  
•Keep-Alive:  
○A scheduled task is configured on A4CB-US-SQL01 to ping 10.0.0.114 every 5 minutes.  
○Purpose: Keeps the tunnel active by ensuring periodic “interesting traffic” traverses the VPN.  
▪If the tunnel is going offline periodically, check that this ping is still running  
Operational Notes  
•Use Case:  
•Loanwell connects to A4CB-US-SQL01 at IP 192.168.25.62.  
•Access is through a SQL client (TablePlus).  
•Service uses TCP port 1433 (Microsoft SQL Server).  
•Service account in use: svc\_loanwell.  
•Monitoring:  
•Meraki Dashboard → Security & SD-WAN → VPN Status.  
•Expect one tunnel green/active at a time.  
•Testing:  
•Ping/trace from SQL01 or FS01 to an AWS host (e.g., EC2 10.0.0.114).  
•Troubleshooting:  
•Use Meraki Event Log (filter: VPN events) to diagnose negotiation failures.  
•Use packet capture on MX WAN with filter icmp host to confirm ESP traffic.

### •Loanwell AWS VPC (Context Only)

Environment Details  
•VPC CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16  
•Gateway: Virtual Private Gateway (VGW)  
•VPN Type: Route-based IPsec, configured with static routing for interoperability.  
Key Requirements (AWS Side)  
•VPN connection must be set to Static routing.  
•Static IP prefix for the VPN must include 192.168.25.0/24.  
•Each subnet containing instances that need VPN access must have route table entries:  
○Destination: 192.168.25.0/24  
○Target: VGW.  
•Security groups and NACLs must allow inbound/outbound traffic from 192.168.25.0/24.

### Caveats

•Limited Visibility: A4CB does not manage or have access to the AWS VPC environment. We cannot confirm  
routing, security group, or NACL changes made there.  
•Responsibility: Loanwell’s technical team must ensure AWS-side adjustments do not negatively impact their  
workloads.  
•Legacy Limitation: AWS does not officially support policy-based VPNs. The tunnel works but requires tightly  
defined selectors (192.168.25.0/24 ↔ 10.0.0.0/16) and static routing rules.

## Windows Updates Issues

Some A4CB workstations experience limitations with Windows updates, restricting updates to certain  
hours or to certain versions of Windows.

## SQL

Adding users to A4CB's SQL environment  
Sign into A4CB-US-SQL01 > Open and sign into SQL Server management Studio.  
•Use Server type: Database Engine  
•Server name: A4CB-US-SQL01  
•Authentication: SQL Server Authentication  
•Creds for SA are in LastPass.

1.  Create the login (server level)  
    •In Object Explorer, expand Security  
    •Right-click Logins → New Login…  
    •Login name: click Search… → switch to your AD domain → pick the user (or group) → OK  
    •Leave Windows authentication selected  
    •(Optional but common) Set Default database to the database they’ll use  
    •Click OK.
2.  Map them to the database + give them permissions (Such as read-only, owner, etc)  
    •Go back to Security → Logins  
    •Right-click the login you just created → Properties  
    •Click User Mapping (left side)  
    •Check the box for YourDatabase (Usually they're just using the "DataGreenLine" DataBase)  
    •In the bottom pane (“Database role membership”), check the intended permission. (e.g., read  
    only would be "DB\_datareader")  
    •Click OK

### EZ3D-I (3D Viewer)

EZ3D-I is a VATech application. It's for viewing 3D scans from the CBCT system.
The system requirements for this application are high, and require dedicated graphics.
System Requirements: Vatech America | FAQ
Some Lenovo mini-desktops come with DisplayPort-mini ports for the dedicated GPU, with onboard
graphics being HDMI/DisplayPort. You should plug into the dedicated GPU on these machines if possible.
This application also requires specific driver-configurations for Nvidia GPUs.
Config requirements: Vatech America | FAQ
1. Update Nvidia Drivers to the latest
2. Open Nvidia Control Panel > Manage 3D Settings
3. Under Global Presets, set the preset to '3D App – Game Development'

### ePNJPoS

Awesome Dental uses card readers purchased from eprocessingnetwork.com to process payments.
They're small card readers that connect via USB. One is on the Check-In computer and one is on the
Check-Out computer.
The card readers are run through a piece of software called ePNJPoS.
A download to that software can be found here.
Our service on this software includes installing it and making sure it runs. We do not have credentials
for this software and do not service it beyond that.
If they are experiencing problems with it not opening or throwing errors, a reinstall should resolve
those problems. They frequently forget their credentials, but since this is a payment system, we do
not store or keep track of those credentials for them.

all.title: ABM Research
all.description: What It Is Activation Lock is an Apple security feature tied to iCloud/Apple ID that prevents a device from being erased and reactivated without the original Apple ID credentials. It kicks in when someone enables Find My on a device. Without a bypass, a locked device is a brick — you cannot enroll it, wipe it cleanly, or reassign it. This article explains how to troubleshoot the issue: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/exchange/groups-and-distribution-lists/microsoft-365-group-does-not-appear-in-outlook Content from article pasted here: To fix the issue, verify the following settings: Make sure that the user is a member of the Microsoft 365 group: In the Exchange admin center (EAC), select Groups, and then select the group name to open a pane that shows group details. In the pane, check the Members tab to see whether the user is listed. If the user isn't listed, add the user as a group member. Note To view a group in Outlook or Outlook on the web,…

all.filename: Activating In-Place Archive & Applying Retention Policies.md
all.title: Activating In-Place Archive & Applying Retention Policies
all.summary: How to activate In-Place Archive and apply/create retention policies
all.body: Content From the Exchange admin center, find the user who needs In-Place Archive activated. From their user blade, navigate to Others, then find Mailbox Archive. Click Manage mailbox archive and then enable Mailbox archive status. After enabling, open Microsoft Purview from the 365 admin console. Select Solutions from the menu then Data Lifecycle Management. Next, select Exchange (legacy) and confirm the right policies are in place. If you need to create bespoke rules, add a policy then ascribe tags. If you view the Default MRM Policy, you'll get a feel for what kind of Retention tags are possible. With the correct policy in place, head back to Exchange and navigate to the user you're editing. From the user blade, select Mailbox then find Retention policy and click Manage mailbox policies.…

all.filename: Apple Business Manager (ABM) MDM Assignment for Existing Computers.md
all.title: Apple Business Manager (ABM) MDM Assignment for Existing Computers
all.summary: Outlines steps to assign ABM MDM management to STEAMe computers which have already gone through the Setup Assistant process.
all.body: Content If the computer is brand new and has not gone through the Setup Assistant (Out of Box Experience), these steps will not apply. Follow the STEAMe PC Setup checklist in that case. STEAMe ABM Adoption process for existing (not new) laptops: Pre-checks and backups Sign into the user’s MacOS profile and check for the following Apple accounts signed into the device (You may find personal accounts or STEAMe accounts) Data stored via iCloud through various apps If the user is utilizing Apple Notes, Contacts, Calendar, etc., that data is typically stored via iCloud. If we change their signed in account, the data could be misplaced. If iCloud data exists that needs to remain, follow Apple’s documented export process to save a backup. (e.g., Apple Notes App: https://support.apple.com/guide/no…

all.title: Apple Business Manager (ABM) Recommendations
all.summary: C22's recommendations for how to use ABM
all.body: Content Apple Business Manager allows ownership and management of Apple devices (Macs and iOS devices) but has a number of different options that must be managed to enable the correct set of controls. All ABM company implementations fall into one of three categories: 1) Physical Ownership Only - Machines are purchased through Apple and automatically added to ABM but the iCloud account the user associates with the machine during setup is NOT under the direct control of the company. This means the company has inventory info on the machine and can prove to Apple that they are the rightful owner of the machine but they CAN'T move the iCloud account from the machine without the password that only the user knows. 2) iCloud Ownership - The company uses an iCloud account that they control on a mac…

all.title: Backup of Desktop and Documents
all.summary: Description on how we setup desktop and documents backup for Mac and PC Laptops/Desktops.
all.body: Content All laptops and desktops should be backed up using either OneDrive or Google Drive. Which one to use is determined by where the client hosts their email. If they host with Google both Macs and PCs should be backed up using Google Drive. If they host with Microsoft, both Macs and PCs should be backed up using OneDrive. Macs and PCs can both be set to backup using either OneDrive or Google Drive. When telling either app to backup Desktop and Documents you MUST be logged in as the user to backup, not the C22Admin account. ---

all.filename: Block Read.ai and other ai bots.md
all.title: Block Read.ai and other ai bots
all.summary: This article explains how to block and revoke access to Enterprise applications in the Entra environment, such as Read.ai
all.body: Content There are a few problems to overcome, specifically with Read.ai and other ai bots. We can block access via the Teams admin center and Entra easily enough, but if a user has already registered with the application, the bot already has the access it needs invade their calls and meetings, perform transcription, read their mail, calendar, etc. So, the solution involves the following Preventing further registration for users moving forward Removing existing users assigned to the application Revoking refresh tokens for all users Revoking all permissions granted to the application for individual users Step-by-step instructions Part 1 Sign into the Teams admin center Teams Apps Manage apps Locate the app Users and groups Edit availability No one Sign into the Entra Admin Center Enterprise…

all.title: Blue Beam PDF Viewer
all.body: Content Here are some options if you are coming across Blue Beam not showing PDFs in the preview window in Outlook 1. Check Bluebeam Plugin Status: Open Bluebeam Administrator: Go to Help Administrator. Verify Plugin Activation: Ensure the checkbox for the affected application (Outlook) is selected and select "Apply". Reopen Outlook: Open Outlook to confirm the plugin is visible. If Still Invisible: The plugin might be disabled within Microsoft Office. 2. Enable Bluebeam Plugin in Microsoft Office (2010 and above): Open Outlook: Go to File Options. Select Add-Ins: Choose Add-Ins in the left column. Manage COM Add-ins: In the Manage dropdown, select COM Add-ins, and click Go. Enable BluebeamOfficeAddIn: Select the BluebeamOfficeAddIn checkbox, and click OK. 3. Set Bluebeam Revu as Default P…

all.title: C22 Triage Rules
all.summary: Guidelines for running triage.
all.body: Content Triage General Rules Inbox should be checked for new emails every 20 minutes Do not take an item that requires you to talk to a user on the phone directly (too easy to get pulled into longer issues) Always check Red/Green system to make sure people have availability before assigning an item Daily Tasks Assign Tickets to Users Confirm User is Aware of Assignment in Teams Color Code Item Drop Item in User's Folder Clean Other Mail Unsubscribe from unnecessary email Check Alerts Folder Daily Clean Junk Email Folder Daily Remind Users of Assigned Folders having high counts of emails ---

all.title: C22AZ Required License
all.summary: License required by all C22AZ users to manage security features in Entra.

all.body: Content The SMTP port for Scan to Email cannot be changed in the interface on some older iR-ADV devices and will default to 25 which cannot be used with HVE. To update the port to 587: You need to get into the service mode on the copier. Press the Settings/Registration, quickly followed by 2 & 8 simultaneously, then Settings/Registration again. Choose Mode list Choose copier Choose option Choose Network Press . It should change to Change the value on SMTPTXPN from 25 to 587. ---

all.title: Conference Room Options
all.summary: Recommendations for different levels of hardware for conference room meeting configurations.
all.body: Content Clarify business needs. There are essentially a few options: 1. Computer on TV with individual or shared logins. The TV is the monitor and a camera/mic is attached to the computer. No computer needed by the end user (it has a dedicated computer) and the connections are consistent. It is less secure because of possible shared logins and apps staying logged in. 2. Conference System. Similar to first option, but only does the business' conferencing software. No login, no shared space, just a pad that you tap to join a video conference when you invite the conference system "room" to the meeting. Consistent connection without the security issues of the first option, but much more expensive. 3. Shared TV and Cam. Like an owl system, essentially the conference room just has a monitor, mic…

all.title: Eaglesoft Cannot Find Server Error
all.summary: Fix for when a single computer cannot contact the EagleSoft server in a dental office
all.body: Content At a larger dental office recently, we had a single computer that would not connect to the Eaglesoft server no matter what. Even after a complete reinstall, it would always get the error that it can’t connect to the server machine. We contacted Eaglesoft and this is a new problem that they have been recently seeing and appears to be related to a recent .net update. The fix 99% of the time is: Navigate to C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v4.0.30319\Config If machine.config is missing from here: Find machine.config.default and make a copy of it named machine.config Open Eaglesoft technical reference and update the server name/IP Verify Eaglesoft connects and works after making these changes ---

They should then select "Share Invite Link" and send it to the helpdesk@c22tech.com address. Once the email is found in the helpdesk inbox we should add it to the eero account found in LastPass. ---

all.body: Content Exchange Online Mailboxes have limits bases on their license. 50G is the norm is most licenses. 100Gthe is the max with Exchange Online Plan 2. The Archive folder is a part of the mailboxes folder tree and counts against the mailboxes size. The In-Place Archive is a cloud space folder that does not count against the overall size of the mailbox, but the emails do not show up in search. This must be turned on in the Exchange Admin Console per mailbox. Exchange Online limits - Service Descriptions | Microsoft Learn ---

all.title: ForeFront Teams Call Queue
all.summary: Call Queue for ForeFront's phone lines through Teams.
all.body: Content ForeFront uses Microsoft Teams as their phone line provider. They have requested the Call Queue to be set up as follows: Caller calls in during business hours is routed to their designated receptionist. If Receptionist is not available, rings to everyone in the office (Who is Teams calling enabled). If no one answers, caller leaves voicemail Voicemail is stored on a Shared Mailbox "ForeFront Voicemail" (forefrontvoicemail@forefrontscs.com). all.title: Google Workspace MFA
all.body: Content Google's Multi-Factor Authentication is under Security Authentication 2-Step Veification. It is applied to the Organizational Unit (OU) You must first setup MFA on your Admin account before you can enable it for the OU The Enforcement Date is applied to the account's creation date. It will affect existing accounts and could lock out Users. New User Enrollment period is applied to account created after the Enforcement date. This give users a chance to setup MFA not on day 1 or for Admins to create users a few weeks in advanced. Default policy is SMS messages. We recommend using the Google Authenticator app as well. ---

all.summary: PPP Events is a Shared Mailbox account within the PPP 365 tenant. Here's how to make sure employees can see it in their Outlook.

By opening Outlook from the PPP 365 tenant, open events@ via the Open Another Mailbox option (top right, click profile badge). Within the events@ mailbox, you can select Calendar from the left pane and using the ellipses (three dots) next to, My Calendar, review Sharing and Permissions. You should see that the group, "Everyone in the organization," has access along with several important PPP users who have mailbox access as well. LastPass includes a root level folder called "Shared-Folder for local admin passwords" Sub folders should exist for each client within this folder. The name of the sub folder should be the client name. If a sub folder doesn't exist, create one. The above location is where these password objects need to be stored. Going forward the following structure should be used to generate and store a local admin password for all desktops: All computers should first be named in LMI with the last 5 characters of their serial number as the end of their name (in LMI only) If the device is physically in your possession the serial number should be listed on the bottom of the machine If you have already logged into the machine you can get the serial number the following ways: In Windows enter the following text into a command window (admin privledges are note required):WMIC BIOS GET SERIALNUMBER In MacOS click on the About button in the main menu button and the serial number should be listed there. ---

all.title: Outlook for Mac Crashing
all.body: Content Outlook for Mac Crashing Helpdesk just a note that I'll be adding to the KB. The legacy version of Outlook crashes when it receives a specific encryption email and prompts the user for their credentials. It stalls out because the sending server (metcapbank in this instance) wants credentials on their tenant, not the receiving server. This is because legacy Outlook doesn't support S/MIME. It stays crashed because the last email you selected is the encrypted email. I was lucky enough that there was an update in Outlook that "refreshed" the email selection, but otherwise, you may have to rebuild a profile to get Outlook to work again. ---

all.title: Outlook Index Search Not Cooperating
all.summary: If a user reports their Outlook search isn't working, here's a tip.
all.body: Content The specific case where this was helpful, a PPP user reported Shared Contacts weren't appearing in Outlook via Search. When trying to search, Outlook throws an indexing error. The resolution did not seem to have anything to do with Windows' indexing tools/services. From Outlook, navigate to File Accounting Settings Next, select the account in question and click Change From the Exchange Account Settings window, select More Settings Toggle to Advanced and uncheck Download shared folders This will ensure Outlook doesn't cache as many items and always looks to the server for information. This will prevent the user from finding certain items when in offline mode, but if searching through a large number of Shared Contacts is vital, CRMs exist for this purpose. ---

all.title: Resync Server Timeclock
all.summary: If your server timeclock isn't syncing properly and seems like it might be locked by GPO, follow these steps.
all.body: Content First, confirm where your machine is getting it's time sync from. Open elevated CMD session and enter the following: w32tm /query /source Especially, in local environments, various machines may need to point to one another. If the goal is to sync to a global standard (NTP), within your elevated CMD session, enter the following: w32tm /config /syncfromflags:manual /manualpeerlist:"pool.ntp.org" /update After running, force an immediate sync with: w32tm /resync Now, confirm your source: w32tm /query /source Bonus: If you're running into issues and Virtual Machines are involved, check their integration services. 1. Login to the machine where your VMs are hosted and open Hyper-V. 2. Right click on each VM and select Settings. 3. Check Integration Services and make sure Time synchroniza…
