all.filename: Sending Same Teams Invite to Multiple (Separate) Parties.md
all.title: Sending Same Teams Invite to Multiple (Separate) Parties
all.summary: When a user needs emails to remain anonymous among groups but wants everyone to join the same Teams meeting, you'll need to follow the steps below.
all.body: Content When creating a Teams meeting in Outlook, if you wish to create a calendar invite yet send the same link to separate parties, you'll need to do a small workaround. Otherwise, when trying to copy and paste the original meeting's information, this will prompt an error message saying that the meeting info you're pasting doesn't match the original data. Step 1) Create meeting as you normally would Step 2) Create separate invites as needed Step 3) In all invites that are not the original, click Don't Host Online from the ribbon Step 4) Copy and paste original meeting information into additional invites Step 5) Send. No error message should prompt. You may need to set the Location. ---

all.title: Server Backup Drive Swap

all.body: Content From the client's Host Server, open Windows Server Backup, and verify the backup you're looking for exists. While the Host most likely has the physical drive connected to it, the backup may not be operating here. (For example, ASNR's drives are connected at the Host, but the backup occurs on the File Share. You will need to log into the File Share to see if the backup was successful.) With Windows Server Backup open, find a successful backup and verify the location. You'll need to make sure the drive remains in this location after swap. If the latest backups have failed, check for a reason. Most common: the current drive letter doesn't match the designated backup letter. I.E., current backup drive is connected to D, but your backups are mapped to F. Once you have the data you need…

This audit type can affirm that an organization’s controls are designed effectively. SOC 2 Type II Includes the same information, with the addition of testing a service organization’s controls over a period of time. Risk Management Organizations need to execute a holistic risk management strategy over performing basic and reactive mitigation. A system should be in place that proactively works to reduce the volume and severity of risks, rather than just responding to them as they appear. all.filename: Stop Network Storm from Multicast Source.md
all.title: Stop Network Storm from Multicast Source
all.summary: Setting in Unifi to stop a multicast device on the network from slowing flooding the switches and making the network unstable
all.body: Content If a Multicast device is sending out too many packets or the packets it sends are echoing on the network you will see devices have diminished performance including issues like being unable to get an IP address from DHCP. In order to stop this issue you need to limit how many packets this device is allowed to send to the network. On a Unif Switch you'll need to find the port you think is causing the issue or set this limiter on all ports (which may cause other issues). Select the port(s) affected and select the Advanced option. Under this section select Storm Control. Once that box is checked you'll see additional options including Unicast, Multicast, and Broadcast. Set all three of these limiters to 300 pkts/second or less. This should stop the storm and stablize the network. If sp…

all.title: vCenter maintenance - Certificate Renewal (Every 18 months)
all.summary: vCenter Machine SSL certificates issued by VMCA expire every 2 years. Renew proactively at the 18-month mark to avoid outages.
all.body: vCenter Machine SSL certificates issued by VMCA expire every 2 years. Renew proactively at the 18-month mark to avoid outages. Content Steps • SSH into vCenter: ssh root@192.168.25.25 • Drop into bash shell: shell • Run the Certificate Manager: /usr/lib/vmware-vmca/bin/certificate-manager • Select Option 3 — Replace Machine SSL certificate with VMCA Certificate • Authenticate with Administrator@vsphere.local credentials • Fill in prompts: Country, Organization, Locality, IP (192.168.25.25), Hostname (vcenter.accionchicago.local) • Press Enter to accept defaults for optional fields (Email, VMCA name) • Wait 2–5 minutes for completion • Restart all services if needed: service-control --stop --all && service-control --start --all Notes • If Certificate Manager reports 'does not support vCente…

all.body: Content In the Word Template Macro remove the display name in Distribution Lists leaving just the email address for group members. The display name sometimes breaks and returns a GUID Add Group Owner as an item when displaying the Distribution List members. Split by spaces since multiple owners can exist for a single Dist List O365 Security Group needs a description O365 Security Group Mail Enabled needs a description For Primary Services only show users that have at least one user ---

all.filename: Whitelist All Domains That Have Been Sent To By Client.md
all.title: Whitelist All Domains That Have Been Sent To By Client
all.summary: The manual process to get a list of all of the domains a client has sent to and then whitelist them in O365
all.body: Content Login to the Exchange Online Admin Portal and go to Mailflow - Message Trace Click Start a Trace and set the Senders to @ , set the time range to 30 Days, set Direction to Outbound, Select the type as Enhanced Summary Report. Click Search and then click Export results- Export all results to download a CSV file. Open the CSV in Excel and delete all columns except RecipientAddress Select the Column A with RecipientAddress and then click Data- Text to Columns. With the Wizard select Delimited - Next, Delimiter should be Other with the value set to @. Click Finish The B Column should now have the list of domains. Select Column A and delete it. Select the new column that should now be column A and click the Remove Duplicates button in the Data ribbon bar. Check My Data Has Headers, sele…

all.filename: Windows Backup Fail_ Writer's Timeout Expired Between Freeze and Thaw Events.md
all.title: Windows Backup Fail: Writer's Timeout Expired Between Freeze and Thaw Events
all.summary: Registry fix to give Windows Server Backup more time to freeze a machine before starting a backup job.
all.body: Content Increase the timeout time for VSS operations: Click on Start Run. Enter: regedit.exe. Ensure backup of registry is made, before making any changes. Find the following registry entry: HKEY LOCAL MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows: NT\CurrentVersion\SPP\CreateTimeout. If the above path is not found, create it. Right-click SPP New DWORD (32 bit) Value. Name: CreateTimeout. In the Value data field, enter: 1200000. This needs to be a Decimal value. 1200000 equals 20 minutes. Reference URL: https://me.n-able.com/s/article/Error-VSS-error-0x800423f2-The-writer-s-timeout-expired-between-the-Freeze-and-Thaw-events-On-Windows-Server-2008 ---

all.title: Windows OS Upgrade Fails
all.summary: Use this guide when your Windows OS upgrade fails, or ends up on Enterprise instead of Pro.
all.body: Content Here's the scenario for why this is getting written up: User purchased a Windows 11 Home tablet. We traversed to Microsoft Store and purchased Windows 11 Pro upgrade. During installation, the upgrade failed and when we restarted the device, Windows was convinced we were on a "non-genuine Windows 10 Enterprise" license. Read: NO BUENO. Where was our original Windows 11 Home license? And what happened to our Windows 11 Pro upgrade? If you run Powershell with the following prompt, you'll most likely find the key you're looking for: (Get-CimInstance -ClassName SoftwareLicensingService).OA3xOriginalProductKey Save that key and let's work towards resolving the disconnect. Next, open CMD prompt (run as administrator) and type the following: slmgr /ipk VK7JG-NPHTM-C97JM-9MPGT-3V66T The abo…
